channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: After investigating Jan. 6, House GOP sides with Trump and goes after Liz Cheney
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Wrapping up their investigation on the Jan. 6 2021 Capitol attack, House Republicans have concluded it's former GOP Rep.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Attack on 2 apparent Venezuelan migrants in Denver area seems gang-related, police chief says
item.description: DENVER (AP) — More than a dozen people tied up, pistol whipped and terrorized a man and woman at a crime-riddled apartment complex in a Denver suburb, the city’s police chief said Tuesday, adding it appeared to be a gang-related attack on two Venezuelan immigrants.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A couple hundred North Korean troops killed, wounded in battles with Ukrainian forces
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A couple hundred North Korean troops fighting alongside Russian forces against Ukraine have been killed or wounded during battle in the Kursk border region, a senior military official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man who killed brother and 3 others set to be put to death in Indiana's first execution in 15 years
item.description: MICHIGAN CITY, Indiana (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 1997 killings of his brother and three other people is set to receive a lethal injection by early Wednesday in the state's first execution in 15 years, without any independent witness present under the state's laws shielding information a
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Walmart testing body cameras for some in-store employees
item.description: Some in-store Walmart employees are wearing body cameras as part of a pilot program, according to a company spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Things to know about the shooting at a Christian school in Wisconsin
item.description: Police in Madison, Wisconsin, are trying to determine what prompted a 15-year-old student to open fire at a private Christian school. The shooting Monday was just days before the winter break. A fellow student and a teacher were killed. The shooter also died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tech consultant found guilty of second-degree murder in stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A San Francisco jury on Tuesday found a tech consultant guilty of second-degree murder in the stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee, which carries a sentence of 16 years to life, rejecting the defendant's claim that he had acted in self defense.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tax brackets and more are changing in 2024, 2025: What to know
item.description: We're more than 100 days out from Tax Day, but there are some impending updates you may want to prepare for.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Congress nears funding deal with more than $100 billion in disaster aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congressional leaders are nearing the unveiling of a spending agreement that will keep the federal government funded through March 14 and provide more than $100 billion in emergency aid to help states and local communities recover from Hurricanes Helene and Milton, and other natura
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Death penalty sought against suspects tied to Mahogany Jackson murder case
item.description: “The facts of this case are deplorable and sickening,” Former BPD Chief Scott Thurmond said at the time. “Saddest of all, they were made public by the suspects’ decision to videotape portions of this horrific act.
item.dc:creator: Drew Taylor, NewsNation

item.title: Family of slain Cash App founder Bob Lee reacts to Nima Momeni verdict
item.description: On Tuesday morning, a San Francisco jury announced it had found Nima Momeni guilty of second degree murder in the death of Cash App founder Bob Lee.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, Fox 5

item.title: Taco Bell adding chicken nuggets to menus nationwide
item.description: Taco Bell, known for its Mexican-inspired cuisine, will begin serving its take on chicken nuggets later this month.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Event promoters, hotels and lodging sites soon will have to disclose extra fees up front
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission on Tuesday said it will soon require hotels, vacation rental platforms and live event promoters to disclose any fees up front when they list prices.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FTC bans junk ticket, hotel fees
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) banned surprise 'junk fees' for ticketing platforms, vacation rental sites and hotel fees on Tuesday. Once in office, Trump could move to withdraw the rule for further consideration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: An Alabama woman is doing well after the latest experimental pig kidney transplant
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An Alabama woman is recovering well after a pig kidney transplant last month that freed her from eight years of dialysis, the latest effort to save human lives with animal organs.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Americans' trust in nation's court system hits record low, survey finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At a time of heightened political division, Americans' confidence in their country’s judicial system and courts dropped to a record low of 35% this year, according to a new Gallup poll.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Suspect in Gilgo Beach serial killings is charged in the death of a seventh woman
item.description: The New York architect accused in a string of deaths known as the Gilgo Beach killings has been charged in the death of a seventh woman.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Drone detectors in New Jersey have found 'little or no evidence' of wrongdoing, governor says
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — Drone-detecting devices deployed in New Jersey in the past week have found “little to no evidence” of anything nefarious or threatening, Gov.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Horse patrol keeps watch over 'roughest patch' of U.S.-Mexico border
item.description: LORDSBURG, New Mexico (Border Report) – Agency lore states that when Congress founded the U.S. Border Patrol in 1924, there were two requisites to join: Grab your gun and bring a horse.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Judge sentences Zephen Xaver, man who executed 5 women at Florida bank, to death
item.description: Zephen Xaver pleaded guilty to five counts of murder for killing Ana Piñon Williams, Debra Cook, Marisol Lopez, Cynthia Watson and Jessica Montague at a SunTrust Bank in Sebring, Florida in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, Fox 5

item.title: Man sentenced for helping pregnant Chinese women travel to give birth in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A California man was sentenced Monday to nearly 3 1/2 years in prison for running an extensive business that helped pregnant Chinese women travel to the United States to deliver babies who would automatically have American citizenship. U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bud Light no longer the best-selling draft beer at bars, data suggests
item.description: The overtaking of Bud Light as America's most popular draft beer has been "years in the making," according to the founder of Draftline Technologies, a company that provides software for beverage distributors.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Lawmakers signal movement toward government funding deal
item.description: Top Republicans are signaling progress in government funding talks as leaders look to clinch a deal ahead of a looming Friday deadline. House Appropriations Chair Tom Cole (R-Okla.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: Former FBI informant pleads guilty to fabricating Biden claims
item.description: A former FBI informant on Monday entered a guilty plea on four federal charges related to falsifying statements that in part spurred a GOP congressional investigation into the Biden family.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump’s Mar-a-Lago press conference
item.description: President-elect Trump gave his first formal press conference since winning election last month, during which he announced a major Softbank investment in the U.S. and fielded a bevy of questions from reporters at Mar-a-Lago.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump, Softbank CEO announce $100B SoftBank investment in the US
item.description: resident-elect Donald Trump will join SoftBank Group CEO Masayoshi Son at his Florida home on Monday to announce that the Japanese company is planning to invest $100 billion in U.S. projects over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Fox 5

item.title: Teacher and student killed, 6 injured in Wisconsin school shooting
item.description: A teacher and a student were killed in a shooting at the Abundant Life Christian School in Madison, Wisconsin, police said Monday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Devin Willems, ABC 4

item.title: LeBron James returns to score 18 in Lakers' win vs Grizzlies after missing 2 games with foot injury
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — LeBron James returned to the Los Angeles Lakers on Sunday night in a 116-110 victory over the Memphis Grizzlies after a two-game break. James is fighting a left foot injury and was away from the team most of last week because of personal reasons.
item.dc:creator: KYLE GLASER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: McConnell urges Trump to reject isolationism
item.description: Outgoing Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) is calling on President-elect Trump to reject isolationists in the Republican Party who will argue for the United States to focus on China at the expense of Ukraine and other areas of the world.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump girds for battle with Democrats, Supreme Court over birthright citizenship
item.description: President-elect Trump, Democratic lawmakers and the Supreme Court are on a collision course over restricting birthright citizenship, which Trump has identified as a top priority for his incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Jalen Hurts accounts for 3 TDs and Eagles beat Steelers 27-13 for franchise-best 10th straight win
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Jalen Hurts absorbed the shots when the Eagles' recent anemic passing attack was panned by his own star wide receivers. Hurts and A.J.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Godiva steps in to help Metropolitan Ministries during holiday rush
item.description: The iconic pink Godiva truck brought treats for families and volunteers, but their real gift was a $25,000 donation to help restock shelves that have been emptying faster than ever.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former longtime South Carolina congressman John Spratt dies at 82
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — John Spratt, a former longtime Democratic congressman from South Carolina who successfully pushed for a balanced budget deal in the 1990s but was unseated decades later when his district turned Republican, has died. He was 82.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US agencies should use advanced technology to identify mysterious drones, Schumer says
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — After weeks of fear and bewilderment about the drones buzzing over parts of New York and New Jersey, elected officials are urging action to identify and stop the mysterious flights. “There’s a lot of us who are pretty frustrated right now,” Rep. Jim Himes, D-Conn.
item.dc:creator: BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS and DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Miami Dolphins wide receiver Grant DuBose taken off the field on a stretcher after hit to the head
item.description: Grant DuBose, who returned Sunday after a shoulder injury sidelined him for part of the season, was listed as being in stable condition.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: California man dies after being mauled by his own dogs: police
item.description: A man was mauled by his three dogs at a neighborhood park Thursday afternoon, according to the San Diego Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Tony Shin, ABC 4

item.title: Jamie Foxx reportedly injured in altercation at Beverly Hills restaurant
item.description: Foxx, who turned 57 on Friday, was dining with family at Mr. Chow when patrons at a nearby table hurled rude and vulgar insults to the "In Living Color" and "Ray" star, a witness told TMZ.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5
